Petition For Order Compelling Former Trustee To File Accounting [Prob. Code 16060, 16061,016062]
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Evan Abrams, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Catherine Rodden, Trustee;
Patrick Waltz, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for and with William Shine, Former Trustee, also appearing by CourtCall;
Ernie Baumberger, Esq. appearing by Zoom;
Caitlin Noble, Deputy Attorney General appearing by Zoom for the People of the State of California.

The Court addresses the Order to Appear. Mr. Waltz responds stating that he was not present on October 12th because he had signed a stipulation regarding the petition, that at page two of that stipulation it states that there wouldn't be a hearing, that he was on a plane to Georgia, and that he would have been at the hearing. Mr. Waltz inquires about jurisdiction. The Court provided an explanation regarding jurisdiction. Following Mr. Waltz' comments, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar.

The Court addresses the petition stating that it went back and looked at everything including Ms. Rodden's amended first account noting that Ms. Rodden did not file for the period that Mr. Shine was Trustee. Mr. Waltz states that it is imperative that his client get all of the records from Ms. Rodden. Mr. Abrams states that their involvement began when they filed a petition for modification to pay out to three charities. Discussions are held. Mr. Waltz addresses the waiver of attorney-client privilege and states that his client has asked his daughter for the records. The Court addresses Mr. Abrams regarding the fact that he never mentioned that his firm represented Mr. Shine for a time period, that he has a duty of candor, and that the Court had to find out at the "third copy". The Court notes that Ms. Rodden and Mr. Shine are adverse. Mr. Abrams states that 1000 pages were sent after October 18th. Further discussions are held. Mr. Abrams notes that his firm does not have the records. The order to produce the records is directed to Ms. Rodden. Mr. Abram states that they will have someone bring the records. Mr. Waltz requests the legal billing invoices and billing records. That order is directed to the Farella firm. The Court states that it is looking at the prayer from the petition, notes that it will grant the first paragraph in the petition, and requested that Ms. Noble email a new proposed order to the clerk.

The Court states that there will be four years of accountings, that it will deal with Mr. Shine's first account for the period of 10/26/14 through 10/25/16, and that the second account period of 10/26/16 through 7/18/18 will follow the first account. Status on the filing of Mr. Shine's first account is set for March 1, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The documents are to be produced by December 20, 2021. The Court continues the petition for settlement and the petition by the Regents of the University of California to compel distribution currently on calendar December 14, 2021 to March 1, 2022. The Court states that both petitions will trail the accountings.

The Court took the Petition for Order Compelling Former Trustee to File Accounting under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-01-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rhonda Goldstein, via ZOOM, counsel for the University of CA, Interested Party; Caitlin Noble, via ZOOM, counsel for the People of the State of CA, Objector; Evan Abrams, via ZOOM, counsel for Catherine Rodden, Trustee.

At the outset, the court queries Ms. Noble regarding notice to former Trustee, Mr. Shine. Ms. Noble reports that notice had been provided to Mr. Shine's attorney. The court advises counsel that there is no authority to waive notice and Mr. Shine must be personally served. Ms. Noble agrees and will serve Mr. Shine with notice. Court and counsel continue to discuss several issues with the petition for approval of settlement including the effect on the additional parties not mentioned in the settlement petition if the court approves the request. Mr. Abrams then provides additional oral argument in support of the petition for approval of settlement. After further discussion, the court orders Ms. Nobel and Mr. Abrams to meet and confer prior to the next calling of this case.

The Accounting Petition is taken off calendar. The Petition for Approval of Settlement is taken under submission. The Petition for Order Compelling Former Trustee to file Accounting is continued to August 24, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Petition For Order Compelling Former Trustee To File Accounting [Prob. Code 16060, 16061,016062]
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Continued To May-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Continued To Mar-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evan Abrams Esq., counsel for Catherine Rodden, Successor Trustee (appearing by telephone); Caitlin Noble, Esq., counsel for California Dept. of Justice, interested person (appearing by telephone).

The Court recites the matters now before the Court and the history of the case. The Court then expresses concern regarding the want of verification and that general allegations are made on information and belief. In general, the Court would not consider an unverified accounting an accounting at all and not one that could be approved.

Ms. Noble echoes the Court's concerns, adding concern as to the fees requested and noting that Ms. Rodden worked for the former trustee [her father] during his tenure as the trustee. As such, she should have personal knowledge and should be able to verify the accounting.
Mr. Abrams responds, denying that Ms. Rodden's employment or that the fact that the former trustee was her father would in any way provide her with sufficient personal knowledge to verify the accounting. He also notes that since the basis for the Attorney General's involvement in the case is occasioned by the former trustee's lack of reporting, a Petition to compel and accounting directed at the former trustee would be required.

The Court concurs with the need for a separate Petition to compel the former trustee to report. The Court continues the current matter to March 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and directs the California Attorney General to file a Petition to Compel Accounting directed to the former trustee for hearing on that date [March 23, 2021].

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Continued To Nov-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams appeared by telephone for Catherine Rodden (Trustee).
Caitlin Noble appeared by telephone for The People of The State of California.

Ms. Noble stated that they have been in settlement negotiation, that they are not there yet and that they have an agreement to make some corrections to the first account. Mr. Abrams stated that Ms. Noble is accurate, that he was informed that the prior Trustee has new Counsel and spoke of having a settlement conference. Ms. Noble stated that Mr. Abrams has not been able to disclose any information regarding the former Trustee's Attorney. The Court stated that this has been here for two years, that it is apparent in the preparation and presentation of this accounting that no one looked at the local rules that require that accountings not exceed a two year period, that this accounting is for half a decade, that there is no support for the Trustee fees and no evidence of the work performed, and that it is unclear what to do with the reference to the Attorney's fees when there is no amount or support for those fees. The Court also stated that the accounting is too broad in scope, that it will not review this accounting, and that they will need to amend the accounting, or file a new first accounting petition for the first two years. In addition, the Court stated that if the parties want to work out the issues on their own they can do so, otherwise they need to submit an accounting that is consistent with the local rule, that it will not babysit this for another two years and that the parties can go to a settlement conference if they want to, but it will not order the parties to a settlement conference this time. Ms. Noble requested that this petition be denied and that the Trustee file an amended petition that complies with the local rules, and stated that she is looking for documents in support of the professional fees, the cash distributions, the Trustee's fees and time records and that she has been asking for them for the last few months. Mr. Abrams stated that he has provided everything they were asked and that he is not sure if Counsel for the prior Trustee has provided anything. The Court stated that it does not know how this Trustee can account for the prior Trustee, that they can either take this petition off calendar and start over, or file an amended petition that covers the period of October 26, 2014 to October 26, 2016. The Court also stated that if it does not see a petition filed by the next calling of the case, it will take this off calendar. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to November 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of an amended petition that complies with the local rules.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To File An Accounting; Declaration Of Deputy Attorney General Shining J. Hsu In Support
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams appeared by telephone for Catherine Rodden (Trustee).
Caitlin Noble appeared by telephone for The State of California.

The Court asks Ms. Nobel if she is satisfied with this accounting. Ms. Nobel stated that she is not satisfied as to the form of the accounting as it does not comply with Local rules 14.59, 14.62, 14.64 and 14.86. Ms. Noble also stated that the accounting for the 2014 through 2018 period appears to be an improper carve out, that Ms. Rodden was not the Trustee at that time and has no personal knowledge, that there are no bank statements to verify the amounts, and that she has been in contact with Counsel for the Former Trustee and is hoping to fill in the gaps. The Court stated that it will not examine a five-year accounting and that they will have to file amended petitions for two-year accounting periods. Ms. Noble stated that the Petitioner is requesting accounting petitions for all years, that she is willing to continue working with Counsel for both Trustees and try and continue to resolve this, and requested that the petition for settlement of this accounting be denied. Ms. Noble also stated that she agrees that this petition moots the petition to compel the accounting, that the bifurcated issue of July 30, 2019 regarding distribution of fees should be deemed moot and requested a 90-day continuance to continue working on it. Mr. Abrams suggested a 30-day continuance. The Frist Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. is continued to September 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to meet and try and work it out. If the parties are unable to work things out, the Court will order that the accountings be filed.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust And For Approval Of Payment Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams for Catherine Rodden (Trustee)
Shining Hsu for the Attorney General of California.

The Court noted that the request for direct distribution to a list of charities may be the appropriate way to go, that the AG indicated that they do not want fees to be dealt with until the accounting is done and questions if there is a dispute as to the hold back. Ms. Hsu stated that the issue is the amount of the hold back. Mr. Abrams stated that they did not have a chance to discuss it. Ms. Hsu stated that because they don't have an accounting they don't have any idea of what's going on, that there are no specifics as to an appropriate amount and that their position is that the Trustee should account before determining specific dollar amounts to address specific fees and expenses. Mr. Abrams stated that they are willing to hold back everything except for the specific gift of $250,000.00. Mr. Abrams is to prepare a form of order and provide it to Ms. Hsu for approval as to form. Ms. Hsu informed the Court that she is leaving the AG's Office and that another one of her Colleagues will be here in her place. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Order Modifying Trust, etc., but is not going to approve distribution from the trust or fees (subject to receipt and review of the order). The issue of distribution and fees is bifurcated and continued to September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust And For Approval Of Payment Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar May-28-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evon Abrams for Catherine Rodden (Petitioner/Trustee).
Shining Hsu for California Attorney General.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not an accounting should be required prior to modification of the trust or after. Ms. Hsu expressed concern about other Trustees stepping in without an accounting having been filed, stated that $326,000.00 have been paid in trustee's fees, that the Attorney General has brought a suit against William Shine in another matter and asked that the petition be denied at this time. Mr. Abrams stated that they are seeking guidance from the Court and that distribution will either be made to the donor advised fund, or directly to the two charities. The Court asks how the Attorney General thinks the trust should be modified, whether the parties want to do mediation or discovery, and if the parties are ready to submit on that issue if the Court severs all other issues other than modification. The Petition for Order Modifying Trust, etc. is continued to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties have met and conferred as to how this petition will proceed, whether the Attorney General has a preference between the proposed method of modification or an alternative form of modification, and that the Attorney General will tell the Court what that is.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust And For Approval Of Payment Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Continued To May-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams for Catherine Rodden (Trustee).
Shining Hsu appeared by telephone for California Attorney General.

The Court asked Ms. Hsu if she is objecting to the petition. Ms. Hsu stated that there is concern, that they have received very little information about the Trust, that this has been an irrevocable trust for nearly five years, and that she has requested information about the inventory and the value of the trust. Ms. Hsu also stated that the current Trustee has been in possession of the trust for nine months now, that the original Trustee had it for four years, that no accounting has been provided and that Counsel has represented to her that an accounting would be forthcoming, but she has not received one. Mr. Abrams stated that on April 19th seventeen hundred pages of documents were provided to the Attorney General, that the idea was to file an accounting and a proposed distribution, but he is waiting on modification, and that it accomplishes two things, distribution of June's Trust and establishing a mechanism to put money in a distributable fund. Ms. Hsu stated that she is still in the process of looking at the information. The Petition for Order Modifying Trust and for Approval of Trustee's Fees is continued to May 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give the Attorneys time to talk and to give the Attorney General time to look at the documents and file objections if needed. Objections if any, are to be filed no later than May 22, 2019. The Court stated that a donor advised fund seems more consistent with the Trustor's intent.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
